⚠️ CRITICAL: Awaiting Sean's explicit authorization. Do not execute without approval.

# Production Promotion Plan: HEAL-33619
## Cx Case Disposition Level Additions/Changes
**Generated by:** CaseOps Production Promotion Specialist
**Issue:** HEAL-33619
**Prior Reference:** HEAL-33249
**Deployment Method:** sf-project-deploy
**Target Sandbox:** 10xhealth-sean
**Status:** PROPOSAL ONLY — No Production changes have been made.

---

## 1. Summary — What Changes in Production

### Metadata Components Affected

| Object | Field/Component | Type | Change Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Case | Disposition_Level_1__c (or equivalent API name) | CustomField — Picklist | Add 1 new value: "Test - At Home Blood"; Rename 5 existing values in-place |
| Case | Disposition_Level_2__c (or equivalent API name) | CustomField — Dependent Picklist | Add ~26 new L2 values mapped to controlling value "Test - At Home Blood"; Rename 2 existing values in-place |
| Case | Disposition_Level_3__c (or equivalent API name) | CustomField — Dependent Picklist | Add L3 values (FCR, Send Info, Escalation, Transfer) mapped to applicable L2 controlling values |
| Case | Record Types (all applicable) | RecordType | Confirm new L1/L2/L3 picklist values are included in record type picklist availability (no new record types created) |

### Renames Applied (in-place, no API value change)
**Level 1 Renames:**
- Blood → Test - Blood
- Gut Health → Test - Gut Health
- MGT → Test - Methylation Genetic
- PGT → Test - Precision Genetic
- Mitochondrial Testing → Test - Mitochondrial

**Level 2 Renames:**
- Tell me about the blood test → Tell me about your products (under Test - Blood)
- Tell me about the gut health test → Tell me about your products (under Test - Gut Health)

### What Does NOT Change
- No Flows
- No Validation Rules
- No Triggers
- No Apex Classes
- No Page Layouts (fields already present in Call Details section — verify only)
- No new objects or fields created
- No Approval Processes
- No Permission Sets

### Blast Radius Assessment
Minimal. Three existing CustomField picklist definitions on the Case object. No logic components touched. Renames are label-only (API stored value preserved by Salesforce rename-in-place method). All other Case configuration is unchanged.

---

## 2. Pre-Deployment Checklist

**ALL items below must be verified and confirmed BEFORE any deployment command is executed.**

### 2.1 Confirm Target Org Connections Are Active

**Check sandbox org (10xhealth-sean) is authenticated and accessible:**
```
sf org display --target-org 10xhealth-sean
```
Expected: Returns org details including Username, Instance URL, and Connected Status. If not authenticated, run `sf org login web --alias 10xhealth-sean` first.

**Check Production org is authenticated and accessible:**
```
sf org display --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns Production org details. API version should be noted. If alias differs from "prod", substitute correct Production org alias in all commands below.

**Verify API version compatibility between sandbox and Production:**
```
sf org display --verbose --target-org 10xhealth-sean | grep -i "api version"
sf org display --verbose --target-org prod | grep -i "api version"
```
Expected: Both orgs on the same or compatible API versions. Mismatch may cause metadata incompatibility.

### 2.2 Verify Current Production State of Affected Fields

**Retrieve current Production metadata for all three disposition fields (read-only retrieval — no changes made):**
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" --target-org prod --output-dir ./pre-deploy-backup/prod
```
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" --target-org prod --output-dir ./pre-deploy-backup/prod
```
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" --target-org prod --output-dir ./pre-deploy-backup/prod
```
⚠️ NOTE: Replace `Disposition_Level_1__c`, `Disposition_Level_2__c`, `Disposition_Level_3__c` with the actual API field names confirmed by Engineering from the Case object metadata. These are placeholder names based on the issue description.

Expected: Three XML files saved to `./pre-deploy-backup/prod/`. Review these files to confirm:
- "Blood", "Gut Health", "MGT", "PGT", "Mitochondrial Testing" exist as current L1 values
- "Test - At Home Blood" is ABSENT (confirming the gap)
- Old L2 names are present and new ones are absent

**Inspect retrieved XML to confirm current Production values:**
```
cat ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml
```
Expected: XML shows existing picklist values without new additions. Archive this file — it is the rollback baseline.

**Query Production Case records using old picklist values to assess rename impact volume:**
```
sf data query --query "SELECT COUNT(Id), Disposition_Level_1__c FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Blood','Gut Health','MGT','PGT','Mitochondrial Testing') GROUP BY Disposition_Level_1__c" --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns row counts per old value. If counts are high (thousands+), Engineering must assess rename risk carefully. If counts are zero, rename risk is negligible. Do not proceed with renames until this count is reviewed and accepted.

**Query Production Case records using old L2 values:**
```
sf data query --query "SELECT COUNT(Id), Disposition_Level_2__c FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_2__c IN ('Tell me about the blood test','Tell me about the gut health test') GROUP BY Disposition_Level_2__c" --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns row counts per old L2 value. Same risk assessment as above.

### 2.3 Confirm Sandbox Test Report Status

Before proceeding, operator must confirm ALL of the following from the Test Report are marked PASS:

- [ ] TC1: "Test - At Home Blood" appears in L1 picklist — PASS
- [ ] TC2: All 25/26 L2 values appear under "Test - At Home Blood" — PASS
- [ ] TC3: L3 values (FCR, Send Info, Escalation, Transfer) present — PASS
- [ ] TC4: L1 rename Blood → Test - Blood confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC5: L1 rename Gut Health → Test - Gut Health confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC6: L1 rename MGT → Test - Methylation Genetic confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC7: L1 rename PGT → Test - Precision Genetic confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC8: L1 rename Mitochondrial Testing → Test - Mitochondrial confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC9: L2 rename under Test - Blood confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC10: L2 rename under Test - Gut Health confirmed — PASS
- [ ] TC11: Dependent picklist isolation (no bleed-over) — PASS
- [ ] TC12: Existing record data integrity post-rename — PASS ← HARD GATE
- [ ] TC13: Christine Greig added as Jira watcher — PASS

⛔ DO NOT PROCEED if TC12 is not confirmed PASS. Data integrity on existing records is a hard gate.

**Open issues from Test Report that must be resolved before deploy:**
- [ ] "National availability (Dependent)" L2 dependency structure clarified with reporter Noah Scott
- [ ] Engineering confirms rename method used is Salesforce rename-in-place (NOT deactivate + new value)
- [ ] HEAL-33249 Sandbox config audited and any stale/partial changes identified
- [ ] Shared L2 label "Tell me about your products" across multiple L1 parents validated in Sandbox with no conflicts

### 2.4 Retrieve and Archive Sandbox Metadata (Deployment Source)

**Retrieve the finalized sandbox metadata for the three fields (this will be the deploy source):**
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./sandbox-deploy-source
```
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./sandbox-deploy-source
```
```
sf project retrieve start --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" --target-org 10xhealth-sean --output-dir ./sandbox-deploy-source
```
Expected: Three XML files in `./sandbox-deploy-source/`. These are the files that will be pushed to Production.

**Manually inspect sandbox XML to confirm all expected values are present before deploying:**
```
cat ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml
```
Confirm: "Test - At Home Blood" is present. All five renamed L1 values appear with new names. Old names are absent.
```
cat ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_2__c.field-meta.xml
```
Confirm: All 26 L2 values under "Test - At Home Blood" are present. Two L2 renames applied.
```
cat ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_3__c.field-meta.xml
```
Confirm: FCR, Send Info, Escalation, Transfer are present and mapped correctly.

### 2.5 Check for Conflicting Pending Deployments in Production

**Check for any active or queued deployments in Production that could conflict:**
```
sf project deploy report --target-org prod
```
Expected: No active deployments in progress. If a deployment is in progress, WAIT until it completes before proceeding.

**Confirm no other team has open changes to Case picklist fields pending in Production:**
Manual check: Coordinate with Engineering lead to confirm no other Salesforce admin or developer has a pending changeset, scratch org deployment, or Gearset deployment targeting Case object picklist fields. This cannot be automated — must be a human coordination step.

### 2.6 Schedule Deployment Window

- Deploy during LOW-TRAFFIC hours (confirm with team lead what constitutes low-traffic for call center operations)
- Notify call center team lead at least 30 minutes before deploying
- Confirm at least one call center agent is available to perform post-deploy validation immediately after deployment completes
- Confirm Engineering lead is available during deployment window to handle any issues

## 3. Promotion Steps — Exact CLI Commands

⚠️ AWAITING SEAN'S EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION. DO NOT EXECUTE WITHOUT APPROVAL.

**Prerequisite confirmation before running any command below:**
- [ ] Sean has explicitly authorized this deployment
- [ ] All Pre-Deployment Checklist items in Section 2 are confirmed
- [ ] All 13 test cases in the Test Report show PASS
- [ ] Deployment window is confirmed and team has been notified
- [ ] Pre-deploy backup files exist in `./pre-deploy-backup/prod/`

### Step 3.1 — Validate Deployment (Dry Run — No Changes Made)

Run a check-only deploy first. This validates the metadata against Production without making any changes. This is mandatory before the real deploy.

```
sf project deploy start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --dry-run \
  --json
```

**What this does:** Runs a validation-only deployment against Production. Salesforce compiles and validates the metadata without committing any changes. Identifies any errors in the deployment package before a live deploy.

**Expected output indicators (success):**
```json
{
  "status": 0,
  "result": {
    "success": true,
    "numberComponentsDeployed": 3,
    "numberComponentErrors": 0
  }
}
```
- `"success": true` — metadata is valid
- `"numberComponentErrors": 0` — no errors
- `"numberComponentsDeployed": 3` — all three fields validated

⛔ If dry run returns ANY errors or `"success": false`, STOP. Do not proceed to Step 3.2. Investigate and resolve errors in Sandbox first. Document errors and return to Engineering.

### Step 3.2 — Execute Live Deployment to Production

Only execute this step after Step 3.1 dry run shows clean success AND Sean has explicitly authorized.

```
sf project deploy start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --json
```

**What this does:** Deploys the three Case CustomField picklist components from the local project (sourced from sandbox retrieval in Section 2.4) to Production. Adds new picklist values and applies renames in-place on all three disposition level fields.

**Expected output indicators (success):**
```json
{
  "status": 0,
  "result": {
    "success": true,
    "numberComponentsDeployed": 3,
    "numberComponentErrors": 0,
    "numberTestErrors": 0
  }
}
```
- `"success": true` — deployment committed to Production
- `"numberComponentsDeployed": 3` — all three fields deployed
- `"numberComponentErrors": 0` — no component errors

⚠️ If deployment returns ANY errors, do not retry automatically. Document the full JSON error output and proceed to Section 5 Rollback immediately.

### Step 3.3 — Record Deployment ID for Audit Trail

From the JSON output of Step 3.2, locate and record the deployment ID:
```
sf project deploy report --target-org prod --json
```
**What this does:** Retrieves the most recent deployment report from Production, including the deployment ID, component list, and status.

**Expected output:** Deployment ID (format: `0Af...`), status `Succeeded`, 3 components listed with no errors.

Save the full JSON output to `./deploy-logs/HEAL-33619-deploy-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json` for audit purposes.

### Step 3.4 — Confirm Record Types Include New Picklist Values (Manual)

This step cannot be performed via CLI alone — it requires Salesforce Setup UI verification.

1. Log in to Production Salesforce as an Administrator
2. Navigate to Setup > Object Manager > Case > Record Types
3. For each Record Type that call center agents use (confirm list with team lead):
   - Click the Record Type name
   - Click "Edit" next to the Picklists section
   - Confirm "Test - At Home Blood" is in the "Selected Values" list for Disposition Level 1
   - Confirm new L2 values are in the "Selected Values" list for Disposition Level 2
   - Confirm L3 values are in the "Selected Values" list for Disposition Level 3
4. If any Record Type is missing new values in its available list, add them manually via the Record Type picklist editor

**Expected:** All applicable Record Types show new values as available. If values are missing from a Record Type after deployment, this is a known Salesforce behavior where new picklist values may not auto-populate into Record Type availability — they must be added manually.

---

## 4. Post-Deployment Validation Steps

All steps below must be executed IMMEDIATELY after deployment completes, before closing the deployment window or notifying the team.

### 4.1 Verify Metadata Deployed Correctly via CLI

**Retrieve the deployed Production metadata and confirm it matches sandbox source:**
```
sf project retrieve start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --output-dir ./post-deploy-validation/prod
```
**Then diff against sandbox source:**
```
diff ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml ./post-deploy-validation/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml
```
```
diff ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_2__c.field-meta.xml ./post-deploy-validation/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_2__c.field-meta.xml
```
```
diff ./sandbox-deploy-source/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_3__c.field-meta.xml ./post-deploy-validation/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_3__c.field-meta.xml
```
Expected: No diff output (files are identical). Any diff output indicates the deployed metadata does not match the intended sandbox configuration — investigate before sign-off.

### 4.2 Verify New L1 Value Exists in Production via SOQL

```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT QualifiedApiName, Label FROM PicklistValueInfo WHERE EntityParticle.EntityDefinition.QualifiedApiName = 'Case' AND EntityParticle.QualifiedApiName = 'Disposition_Level_1__c'" \
  --target-org prod
```
⚠️ Note: `PicklistValueInfo` availability depends on Salesforce API version and org settings. If this query is not supported, use the diff approach in Step 4.1 as the primary validation, plus the manual UI steps in 4.3.

Alternative query approach to check for renamed values on existing records:
```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c = 'Test - At Home Blood'" \
  --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns 0 (no existing records have this brand-new value yet — this confirms the field accepts the new value as a valid API value).

```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c = 'Blood'" \
  --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns 0. If the old value "Blood" still appears on any record after rename, the rename-in-place did not propagate — flag as High severity issue.

```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Blood','Gut Health','MGT','PGT','Mitochondrial Testing')" \
  --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns 0 for all old values. Non-zero count indicates rename did not fully propagate — flag immediately.

```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Test - Blood','Test - Gut Health','Test - Methylation Genetic','Test - Precision Genetic','Test - Mitochondrial')" \
  --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns the same record counts as the pre-deployment query from Section 2.2 (the records that previously had old values should now show new values).

### 4.3 Manual UI Validation in Production (Call Center Agent Perspective)

Have a call center agent (or team lead) perform the following in Production immediately after deployment:

**Validation A — New L1 Value:**
1. Open any Case record in Production
2. Navigate to Call Details section
3. Click Disposition Level 1 picklist
4. Confirm "Test - At Home Blood" appears as a selectable option
5. Confirm "Blood" does NOT appear (renamed to "Test - Blood")

**Validation B — Dependent L2 Values:**
1. Select "Test - At Home Blood" for Disposition Level 1
2. Click Disposition Level 2 picklist
3. Confirm all expected L2 values are visible (spot-check at minimum: "Tell me about your products", "Schedule Test Pickup", "National availability")

**Validation C — L3 Values:**
1. With L1 = "Test - At Home Blood" and any valid L2 selected
2. Click Disposition Level 3 picklist
3. Confirm FCR, Send Info, Escalation, Transfer are present

**Validation D — Renamed Values on Existing Records:**
1. Pull up a Case record that previously had Disposition Level 1 = "Blood"
2. Confirm field now displays "Test - Blood"
3. Confirm no blank/null/error state on the field

**Validation E — No Bleed-Over:**
1. Select "Test - Gut Health" for Disposition Level 1
2. Open L2 picklist
3. Confirm only Gut Health-relevant L2 values appear; "Test - At Home Blood" L2 values do NOT appear under this L1

### 4.4 Verify Christine Greig is a Jira Watcher

1. Open Jira ticket HEAL-33619
2. Navigate to Watchers section
3. Confirm Christine Greig is listed
4. If not present: Add Christine Greig as watcher manually in Jira — this is not a Salesforce action and does not require CLI

### 4.5 Notify Reporter

Once all validations pass:
1. Update HEAL-33619 in Jira with deployment completion note
2. Notify Noah Scott (reporter) that changes are live in Production and request confirmation from call center team
3. Ask Noah Scott to have the team perform a spot-check in Production and reply with explicit confirmation that the issue is resolved

---

## 5. Rollback Plan

⚠️ Execute rollback ONLY if post-deployment validation reveals a critical issue. Do not rollback preemptively.

### Rollback Decision Criteria

Trigger rollback if ANY of the following are observed post-deployment:
- Existing Case records show blank, null, or "[Value no longer exists]" on renamed picklist fields
- Agents cannot select new L1/L2/L3 values from the picklist
- L2 values are bleeding across incorrect L1 parents
- Production deployment resulted in component errors and partial deployment occurred
- Any unexpected field behavior reported by call center agents

### Rollback Step 1 — Assess Scope of Issue

Before rolling back, determine whether the issue is:
- **Label only (rename issue):** Can be fixed by re-renaming in Production directly without full rollback
- **Structural (new values missing or misconfigured):** Requires metadata re-deploy
- **Data corruption (existing records broken):** Requires immediate rollback AND data repair assessment

```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c = null" \
  --target-org prod
```
If null count is significantly higher than pre-deployment baseline, data corruption has occurred — escalate immediately to Engineering before proceeding with any rollback steps.

### Rollback Step 2 — Redeploy Pre-Deployment Backup Metadata to Production

This redeploys the exact Production metadata state captured in Section 2.2 before any changes were made.

**Confirm backup files exist:**
```
ls -la ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/
```
Expected: Three XML files present (Disposition_Level_1__c, Level_2__c, Level_3__c).

**Dry run rollback first:**
```
sf project deploy start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --dry-run \
  --json
```
⚠️ Before running this, ensure your local project directory is pointing to the `./pre-deploy-backup/prod/` XML files, NOT the sandbox-deploy-source files. Copy backup files to your project source directory:
```
cp ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml ./force-app/main/default/objects/Case/fields/
cp ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_2__c.field-meta.xml ./force-app/main/default/objects/Case/fields/
cp ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_3__c.field-meta.xml ./force-app/main/default/objects/Case/fields/
```
Then run the dry-run rollback command above.

**Execute live rollback deploy (only after dry run succeeds):**
```
sf project deploy start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --json
```
Expected: `"success": true`, 3 components deployed, 0 errors.

### Rollback Step 3 — Alternative: Deactivate New Values Only (Partial Rollback)

If the renames are fine but the NEW values ("Test - At Home Blood" and its L2/L3 dependents) are causing issues, a partial rollback can deactivate new values without re-deploying the full pre-backup state:

1. In Production Setup > Object Manager > Case > Fields & Relationships > Disposition Level 1
2. Click "Edit" on the picklist values
3. Find "Test - At Home Blood"
4. Click "Deactivate" (NOT Delete — deactivate preserves any records that may have used the value)
Expected: "Test - At Home Blood" no longer appears as a selectable option for new records but existing records are unaffected.

Repeat for any new L2/L3 values under "Test - At Home Blood" that need to be deactivated.

Note: This partial rollback approach CANNOT undo renames. If renames need to be reversed, full metadata rollback in Step 5.2 is required.

### Rollback Step 4 — Verify Rollback Completed Successfully

**Retrieve post-rollback Production metadata and diff against original backup:**
```
sf project retrieve start \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_2__c" \
  --metadata "CustomField:Case.Disposition_Level_3__c" \
  --target-org prod \
  --output-dir ./post-rollback-validation/prod
```
```
diff ./pre-deploy-backup/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml ./post-rollback-validation/prod/objects/Case/fields/Disposition_Level_1__c.field-meta.xml
```
Expected: No diff output — Production is now identical to pre-deployment state.

**Re-run data integrity query to confirm old values are restored:**
```
sf data query \
  --query "SELECT COUNT(Id), Disposition_Level_1__c FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Blood','Gut Health','MGT','PGT','Mitochondrial Testing') GROUP BY Disposition_Level_1__c" \
  --target-org prod
```
Expected: Returns same record counts as observed pre-deployment (records that previously had these values are restored).

### Rollback Step 5 — Post-Rollback Notifications

1. Update HEAL-33619 in Jira: note that rollback was executed, reason for rollback, and current Production state
2. Notify Noah Scott (reporter) that deployment was rolled back and the issue is still pending — provide estimated timeline for re-investigation
3. Notify Engineering lead with full error output from the failed deployment and rollback confirmation
4. Do NOT close HEAL-33619 — reopen for Engineering investigation of root cause before re-attempting deployment

---

## 6. Sign-Off Criteria

Production is considered SAFE and HEAL-33619 is ready to close ONLY when ALL of the following criteria are met with explicit confirmation:

### Technical Criteria (CLI-verified)

- [ ] **Metadata deployment succeeded:** `sf project deploy report` shows `"success": true`, 3 components, 0 errors
- [ ] **Old L1 values absent from existing records:** SOQL query `SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Blood','Gut Health','MGT','PGT','Mitochondrial Testing')` returns **0**
- [ ] **Renamed L1 values present on migrated records:** SOQL query `SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c IN ('Test - Blood','Test - Gut Health','Test - Methylation Genetic','Test - Precision Genetic','Test - Mitochondrial')` returns **same count as pre-deployment baseline**
- [ ] **Post-deploy metadata matches sandbox source:** `diff` between sandbox-deploy-source and post-deploy-validation XML files shows **no difference**
- [ ] **No null/blank field explosion:** `SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE Disposition_Level_1__c = null` count is **not materially higher than pre-deployment baseline**

### Functional Criteria (Manual UI-verified by call center agent)

- [ ] **New L1 value selectable:** "Test - At Home Blood" appears in Disposition Level 1 picklist on a live Production Case record ✓
- [ ] **All L2 values load correctly:** Selecting "Test - At Home Blood" reveals correct dependent L2 values (spot-check minimum 5 values) ✓
- [ ] **L3 values selectable:** FCR, Send Info, Escalation, Transfer appear in Disposition Level 3 picklist ✓
- [ ] **Renamed L1 values display correctly:** "Test - Blood", "Test - Gut Health", "Test - Methylation Genetic", "Test - Precision Genetic", "Test - Mitochondrial" all appear in L1 picklist; old names are absent ✓
- [ ] **Renamed L2 values display correctly:** "Tell me about your products" appears under both Test - Blood and Test - Gut Health; old L2 names are absent ✓
- [ ] **No bleed-over:** L2 options under each L1 are isolated to their correct parent ✓
- [ ] **Existing records display correctly:** A sample of Case records previously using old picklist values now shows new renamed values with no blank/error state ✓
- [ ] **Record Types confirmed:** All applicable Record Types show new L1/L2/L3 values as available in their picklist selections ✓

### Administrative Criteria

- [ ] **Christine Greig added as Jira watcher on HEAL-33619** ✓
- [ ] **Reporter confirmation received:** Noah Scott (or designated call center team lead) has explicitly confirmed in writing (Jira comment or email) that the disposition picklist values are correct and the team is unblocked ✓
- [ ] **Deployment ID recorded:** Gearset or sf CLI deployment ID logged in HEAL-33619 Jira ticket for audit trail ✓
- [ ] **Rollback baseline archived:** Pre-deployment backup XML files retained in `./pre-deploy-backup/prod/` and stored in a shared location accessible to Engineering for 90 days ✓
- [ ] **Open issues resolved or accepted:** National availability dependency clarified; rename method confirmed; HEAL-33249 audit complete ✓

### Final Gate

> Production is declared SAFE and HEAL-33619 is eligible for closure ONLY when all checkboxes above are confirmed. Reporter sign-off (Noah Scott) confirming the team is unblocked is the final required confirmation before ticket closure.
